question: I have never heard of Intelligent Robotics. What is this degree about?
expected_answer: The Bachelor of Science (Honours) Intelligent Robotics is a 3-year programme that strikes an exquisite balance between the fundamentals of engineering and hands-on practical skills. This multidisciplinary programme combines electronics, robotics, artificial intelligence, automation, and computer programming. It adopts a modern learning approach with early exposure to real-world applications. Graduates will be agile knowledge workers in the IR4.0 age and beyond, highly sought after by the industry.

question: What does a robotics engineer normally do?
expected_answer: A robotics engineer designs and builds machines for automated jobs; the broader field connects mechanical, electrical, software, AI, and automation work.

question: Is robotics an interdisciplinary career?
expected_answer: Yes. Robotics commonly combines mechanical engineering, electrical engineering, computer science, AI, sensors, hardware, and software.

question: What is BYOC in simple words?
expected_answer: BYOC means Build Your Own Curriculum. MMU BYOC lets eligible students choose open electives; BYOC courses can be taken standalone or as part of a track.

question: Why does this course have BYOC electives?
expected_answer: The programme structure lists Elective 1-3 BYOC in Year 3, so BYOC gives students elective choice inside the degree structure.

question: Do BYOC subjects add extra credit hours?
expected_answer: MMU BYOC FAQ says BYOC does not add extra credit hours beyond the programme graduation requirement unless the student consults the programme coordinator for additional credits.

question: How many BYOC credit hours are needed for a track?
expected_answer: To complete a BYOC track, MMU says students need 9 credit hours of open elective courses within the same track.

question: Is a BYOC track the same as a minor?
expected_answer: MMU BYOC FAQ says a BYOC track is not equivalent to a degree minor; a BYOC track needs 9 credit hours, while a minor usually needs more.

question: How do I check if I am eligible for BYOC?
expected_answer: MMU says students should read their academic programme structure, match open elective slots with the BYOC planner, check seat availability, and register through CLiC.

question: Can I choose BYOC courses from outside robotics?
expected_answer: MMU BYOC FAQ says open electives can be chosen from BYOC open elective courses, subject to programme structure and coordinator guidance.

question: Will BYOC appear on my transcript?
expected_answer: MMU BYOC FAQ says BYOC open elective courses are reflected in the academic transcript, and earned BYOC tracks from academic year 2023/2024 onward receive special notation.

question: As a complete beginner, what is BYOC in simple words?
expected_answer: BYOC means Build Your Own Curriculum. MMU BYOC lets eligible students choose open electives; BYOC courses can be taken standalone or as part of a track.

question: As a complete beginner, why does this course have BYOC electives?

question: As a complete beginner, do BYOC subjects add extra credit hours?

question: As a complete beginner, how many BYOC credit hours are needed for a track?
expected_answer: To complete a BYOC track, MMU says students need 9 credit hours of open elective courses within the same track.

question: As a complete beginner, is a BYOC track the same as a minor?

question: As a complete beginner, can I choose BYOC courses from outside robotics?
